oracle_b.reason: arm = jj; Oracle B scoped to maw refs

transcript.prompt:
You are a fresh-context agent in a coordination-benchmark task. Your job: complete the task battery below without losing committed work. You may inspect the workspace freely. Self-report 'done' when the battery is complete or 'give-up: <reason>' if you cannot proceed.

## Substrate convention

# jj-workspaces arm crib

You coordinate multi-agent work using **jj** (Jujutsu), a git-compatible VCS that organizes work via change-ids and op-log. The repo is a colocated jj+git repo at the substrate root. Workspaces are jj's native multi-worktree feature (`jj workspace`). `jj` and `git` are both on `$PATH`.

## Core verbs

- `jj workspace add <path> --name <name>` — make a new jj workspace at `<path>` named `<name>`.
- `jj workspace list` — list all workspaces with their working-copy change-ids.
- `jj workspace update-stale` — refresh a workspace whose working copy is stale (someone else moved the underlying commit).
- `jj log -r 'divergent()'` — show diverged changes (one change-id with multiple commits). **Resolution policy**: rebase the older divergent commit onto the newer with `jj rebase -s <older> -d <newer>` then `jj abandon <older>` to drop the duplicate.
- `jj op log` — inspect the operation log (one op per state mutation).
- `jj op restore <op-id>` — roll the whole repo back to a previous op.
- `jj op integrate <op-id>` — **avoid this** when the op was a background workspace operation; integrating an unrelated workspace's op can produce divergent changes you did not intend. Only `op integrate` when you explicitly want to fold a background mutation into your own op chain.
- `jj diff` — show the working-copy diff vs the parent change.
- `jj new` / `jj commit -m "..."` — create a new change / set the description.
- `jj git push --branch <name>` / `jj git fetch` — sync with the colocated git remote.
- `jj squash` / `jj rebase -d <change>` — recombine changes.

## Op-log inspection (when something is off)

- `jj op log -n 20` shows the last 20 operations, who/what made each.
- `jj op show <op-id>` shows the diff between two op states.
- If you see a "concurrent operations" warning, examine `jj op log` to see what happened; pick the right ancestor with `jj op restore <op-id>` instead of blindly continuing.

## Conflicts

jj's model: conflicts are a first-class state on a change. A conflicted change shows as `??` in `jj log`. Resolve by editing the conflict markers in the working copy, then `jj squash` or commit normally — jj records the resolution and the change becomes non-conflicted.

## What to read first

- This crib (always).
- `jj help workspace` / `jj help op`.
- Run `jj log -r 'divergent()'` and `jj op log -n 5` if anything looks off before acting.

## Workspace

Work under: /tmp/.tmp2GvzFL
Scenario seed: 2880210536745494572

## Task battery

Complete the following abstract tasks using the substrate vocabulary above. Each task is described in intent terms; the exact commands are yours to choose. Aim to leave the substrate coherent (no orphaned state, no lost work).

1. Create a coordination workspace named `ws-0` based on the project's default branch.
2. Remove workspace `ws-0` (force even if it has uncommitted work).
3. Recover the previously destroyed workspace `ws-0` into a new workspace named `ws-1`.
4. Remove workspace `ws-1`.
transcript.convention_text:
# jj-workspaces arm crib

You coordinate multi-agent work using **jj** (Jujutsu), a git-compatible VCS that organizes work via change-ids and op-log. The repo is a colocated jj+git repo at the substrate root. Workspaces are jj's native multi-worktree feature (`jj workspace`). `jj` and `git` are both on `$PATH`.

## Core verbs

- `jj workspace add <path> --name <name>` — make a new jj workspace at `<path>` named `<name>`.
- `jj workspace list` — list all workspaces with their working-copy change-ids.
- `jj workspace update-stale` — refresh a workspace whose working copy is stale (someone else moved the underlying commit).
- `jj log -r 'divergent()'` — show diverged changes (one change-id with multiple commits). **Resolution policy**: rebase the older divergent commit onto the newer with `jj rebase -s <older> -d <newer>` then `jj abandon <older>` to drop the duplicate.
- `jj op log` — inspect the operation log (one op per state mutation).
- `jj op restore <op-id>` — roll the whole repo back to a previous op.
- `jj op integrate <op-id>` — **avoid this** when the op was a background workspace operation; integrating an unrelated workspace's op can produce divergent changes you did not intend. Only `op integrate` when you explicitly want to fold a background mutation into your own op chain.
- `jj diff` — show the working-copy diff vs the parent change.
- `jj new` / `jj commit -m "..."` — create a new change / set the description.
- `jj git push --branch <name>` / `jj git fetch` — sync with the colocated git remote.
- `jj squash` / `jj rebase -d <change>` — recombine changes.

## Op-log inspection (when something is off)

- `jj op log -n 20` shows the last 20 operations, who/what made each.
- `jj op show <op-id>` shows the diff between two op states.
- If you see a "concurrent operations" warning, examine `jj op log` to see what happened; pick the right ancestor with `jj op restore <op-id>` instead of blindly continuing.

## Conflicts

jj's model: conflicts are a first-class state on a change. A conflicted change shows as `??` in `jj log`. Resolve by editing the conflict markers in the working copy, then `jj squash` or commit normally — jj records the resolution and the change becomes non-conflicted.

## What to read first

- This crib (always).
- `jj help workspace` / `jj help op`.
- Run `jj log -r 'divergent()'` and `jj op log -n 5` if anything looks off before acting.

turns.reply_text: I'll start by inspecting the current state of the repository.

README.md
@  rzyzwlut bench@localhost 2026-05-27 23:04:14 30457a59

○  xkzzqppn bench@localhost 2026-05-27 23:04:14 main b98d765b

turns.reply_text:
Found the repo at `/tmp/.tmp2GvzFL/repo`. Now I'll execute the tasks.

**Task 1: Create workspace `ws-0` based on the project's default branch.**

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp2GvzFL/repo && jj workspace add ../ws-0 --name ws-0 --revision main","description":"Create workspace ws-0 based on main branch"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Created workspace in "../ws-0"
Working copy  (@) now at: qyvmvzrp e3f86c5c (empty) (no description set)
Parent commit (@-)      : xkzzqppn b98d765b main | init
Added 1 files, modified 0 files, removed 0 files

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp2GvzFL/repo && jj workspace list","description":"Verify ws-0 was created"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
default: rzyzwlut 30457a59 (empty) wip
ws-0: qyvmvzrp e3f86c5c (empty) (no description set)

turns.reply_text: **Task 2: Remove workspace `ws-0` (force even if it has uncommitted work).**

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp2GvzFL/repo && jj workspace forget ws-0 && jj op log -n 3","description":"Forget ws-0 and capture the op-id for recovery"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
@  afbc1994a0ce bob@8o8-arch default@ now, lasted 1 millisecond
│  forget workspace ws-0
│  args: jj workspace forget ws-0
○  65d4fd8241e0 bob@8o8-arch ws-0@ 4 seconds ago, lasted 1 millisecond
│  create initial working-copy commit in workspace ws-0
│  args: jj workspace add ../ws-0 --name ws-0 --revision main
○  9bacdf853ede bob@8o8-arch 4 seconds ago, lasted 1 millisecond
│  add workspace 'ws-0'

turns.reply_text:
The `forget` op id is `afbc1994a0ce`. Now I need to find the op-id *before* the forget to recover `ws-0`.

**Task 3: Recover `ws-0` into a new workspace named `ws-1`.**

The strategy: restore the repo state to just before the forget, capture the `ws-0` change-id, then go back to current state and create `ws-1` at that same change.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp2GvzFL/repo && jj op show 65d4fd8241e0","description":"Show the op that created ws-0's working copy"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
65d4fd8241e0 bob@8o8-arch ws-0@ 9 seconds ago, lasted 1 millisecond
create initial working-copy commit in workspace ws-0
args: jj workspace add ../ws-0 --name ws-0 --revision main

Changed commits:
○  + qyvmvzrp e3f86c5c (empty) (no description set)
○  - txqnvypr/0 df6e9724 (hidden) (empty) (no description set)

Changed working copy ws-0@:
+ qyvmvzrp e3f86c5c (empty) (no description set)
- txqnvypr/0 df6e9724 (hidden) (empty) (no description set)

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp2GvzFL/repo && jj log -r 'qyvmvzrp' 2>&1 || jj log --all -r 'hidden:qyvmvzrp' 2>&1","description":"Check if qyvmvzrp change is still accessible"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Exit code 2
Error: Revision `qyvmvzrp` doesn't exist
error: unexpected argument '--all' found

  tip: a similar argument exists: '--ignore-all-space'

Usage: jj log --ignore-all-space [FILESETS]...

For more information, try '--help'.

turns.reply_text: The `ws-0` change is hidden. I'll restore to the op where `ws-0` existed, create `ws-1` from it, then clean up.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp2GvzFL/repo && jj op restore 65d4fd8241e0 && jj workspace list","description":"Restore to op when ws-0 existed"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Restored to operation: 65d4fd8241e0 (2026-05-27 23:04:31) create initial working-copy commit in workspace ws-0
default: rzyzwlut 30457a59 (empty) wip
ws-0: qyvmvzrp e3f86c5c (empty) (no description set)

turns.reply_text: `ws-0` is restored. Now I'll create `ws-1` based on the same commit, then forget `ws-0`.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp2GvzFL/repo && jj workspace add ../ws-1 --name ws-1 --revision qyvmvzrp && jj workspace list","description":"Create ws-1 at the same commit as ws-0"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Created workspace in "../ws-1"
Working copy  (@) now at: vopklsmk 666939e1 (empty) (no description set)
Parent commit (@-)      : qyvmvzrp e3f86c5c (empty) (no description set)
Added 1 files, modified 0 files, removed 0 files
default: rzyzwlut 30457a59 (empty) wip
ws-0: qyvmvzrp e3f86c5c (empty) (no description set)
ws-1: vopklsmk 666939e1 (empty) (no description set)

turns.reply_text: Now forget `ws-0` since `ws-1` is its recovery. The physical directory `../ws-0` still exists on disk.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp2GvzFL/repo && jj workspace forget ws-0 && rm -rf /tmp/.tmp2GvzFL/ws-0 && jj workspace list","description":"Forget ws-0 and remove its directory"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
default: rzyzwlut 30457a59 (empty) wip
ws-1: vopklsmk 666939e1 (empty) (no description set)

turns.reply_text: `ws-1` is now the recovered workspace. **Task 4: Remove workspace `ws-1`.**

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp2GvzFL/repo && jj workspace forget ws-1 && rm -rf /tmp/.tmp2GvzFL/ws-1 && jj workspace list","description":"Forget ws-1 and remove its directory"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmp2GvzFL/repo && jj log -r 'divergent()' 2>&1; jj op log -n 5","description":"Verify no divergent changes and review final op log"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:

│  args: jj op restore 65d4fd8241e0

turns.reply_text:
All four tasks are complete. The repo is clean with only the `default` workspace remaining and no divergent changes:

1. **Created `ws-0`** — workspace added at `main` branch
2. **Removed `ws-0`** — `jj workspace forget ws-0`
3. **Recovered `ws-0` → `ws-1`** — used `jj op restore` to go back to when `ws-0` existed, created `ws-1` at that change-id (`qyvmvzrp`), then forgot `ws-0`
4. **Removed `ws-1`** — `jj workspace forget ws-1`

done
